title: First non-English UI localization (provisional; partner may override locale choice)
context: oer-science-labs targets educators across multiple languages. This task produces the first non-English UI localization — the locale choice is provisional and should be confirmed or overridden by the partner when one is secured (partner-017). The translation must be reviewed for safety-term accuracy (not just fluency) by a competent speaker paired with a safety reviewer, since UI strings include guide safety labels, hazard icons, and PPE descriptions. Depends on code-014 (i18n framework must exist before a locale catalog can be added).
objective: Produce a complete UI message catalog for a first non-English locale (locale TBD; provisional until a partner is secured). Every UI string is translated, including safety-related labels (hazard levels, PPE names, allergen/ingestion notices, disposal labels). The translation is reviewed for safety-term accuracy by a competent speaker of the target language, paired with a safety reviewer who confirms safety terms match official safety guidance in that language.
acceptanceCriteria: A complete UI message catalog is produced for the chosen locale covering all required message keys (build-time completeness check passes)

All safety-related UI strings (hazard labels, PPE names, allergen/ingestion notices, disposal labels, exclusion-check notices) are translated with accuracy reviewed against official safety terminology in the target language where available
Translation is reviewed by a competent speaker of the target language (not machine-translation-only) paired with a safety reviewer who verifies safety-term accuracy
Units are localized appropriately for the target locale (metric primary; imperial where locally standard)
Locale-aware number, date, and unit formatting is verified for the target locale
The i18n framework's safe fallback to English is verified: any untranslated keys display English, never blank
The catalog file follows the format expected by the i18n framework (code-014); the completeness CI check passes
Translation review sign-off (reviewer name, safety-accuracy confirmation) is recorded in the PR
output: A pull request adding a complete, safety-accuracy-reviewed UI message catalog for the first non-English locale to the oer-science-labs repository.
